projects.role: Provider, Deployer, Product manufacturer, Distributor, Importer

assessmentTools.name: Prompt Injection Detector
assessmentTools.description: Detects and prevents prompt injection attacks in AI systems. Analyzes input prompts to identify potential security vulnerabilities including malicious instructions, data exfiltration attempts, and unauthorized command execution.

assessmentTools.name: Security Frameworks
assessmentTools.description: Implements comprehensive security frameworks and best practices for AI systems. Provides structured approach to cybersecurity including threat modeling, vulnerability assessment, and security controls implementation.

assessmentTools.name: Grype Vulnerability Scanner
assessmentTools.description: Scans container images and filesystems for known vulnerabilities in dependencies and libraries. Identifies security risks in AI system components.

assessmentTools.name: Safety (PyUp)
assessmentTools.description: Checks Python dependencies for known security vulnerabilities and suggests updates. Maintains secure software supply chain for Python-based AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Snyk
assessmentTools.description: Developer-first security platform that finds and automatically fixes vulnerabilities in code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure as code.

assessmentTools.name: OSS Scorecard
assessmentTools.description: Assesses security posture of open-source projects using automated checks. Evaluates security practices of dependencies used in AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA)
assessmentTools.description: Conducts comprehensive assessment of AI system impact on fundamental rights including privacy, non-discrimination, and human dignity.

assessmentTools.name: Data Classification
assessmentTools.description: Automatically classifies and labels sensitive data including personal data, special categories of data, and confidential information.

assessmentTools.name: GDPR Compliance
assessmentTools.description: Ensures AI systems comply with GDPR requirements including lawful basis, data minimization, purpose limitation, and data subject rights.

assessmentTools.name: PII Detection
assessmentTools.description: Automatically detects personally identifiable information in data sets and documents to ensure proper data protection measures.

assessmentTools.name: Anonymization Checker
assessmentTools.description: Validates effectiveness of data anonymization and pseudonymization techniques to prevent re-identification risks.

assessmentTools.name: Consent Management
assessmentTools.description: Manages user consent collection, storage, and validation in compliance with GDPR and AI Act transparency requirements.

assessmentTools.name: Bias Detection
assessmentTools.description: Identifies and measures biases in AI models and training data across protected characteristics including race, gender, age, and disability.

assessmentTools.name: Fairness Metrics
assessmentTools.description: Calculates statistical fairness metrics to assess model performance across different demographic groups.

assessmentTools.name: Fairlearn
assessmentTools.description: Open-source toolkit for assessing and improving fairness of machine learning models with mitigation algorithms.

assessmentTools.name: AI Fairness 360 (AIF360)
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive toolkit containing metrics and algorithms for bias detection and mitigation across the AI lifecycle.

assessmentTools.name: LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations)
assessmentTools.description: Provides local explanations for individual predictions by approximating complex models with interpretable ones.

assessmentTools.name: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)
assessmentTools.description: Game-theoretic approach to explain machine learning model outputs using Shapley values from cooperative game theory.

assessmentTools.name: Model Cards
assessmentTools.description: Structured documentation framework for reporting model performance, limitations, and ethical considerations.

assessmentTools.name: Data Sheets for Datasets
assessmentTools.description: Documentation framework for dataset characteristics, collection methodology, and recommended uses.

assessmentTools.name: Toxicity Detection
assessmentTools.description: Identifies toxic, harmful, or offensive content in AI system outputs and training data.

assessmentTools.name: Hallucination Detection
assessmentTools.description: Detects when AI models generate false or fabricated information not grounded in training data or context.

assessmentTools.name: Adversarial Robustness Testing
assessmentTools.description: Tests AI model resilience against adversarial attacks and malicious input perturbations.

assessmentTools.name: Model Monitoring
assessmentTools.description: Continuous monitoring of AI model performance, data drift, and concept drift in production environments.

assessmentTools.name: Audit Logging
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive logging system for AI system decisions, inputs, outputs, and user interactions.

assessmentTools.name: Version Control
assessmentTools.description: Tracks and manages different versions of AI models, datasets, and system configurations.

assessmentTools.name: Risk Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Systematic identification, analysis, and management of risks associated with AI system deployment and operation.

assessmentTools.name: Human Oversight
assessmentTools.description: Ensures meaningful human oversight and intervention capabilities in AI system operations.

assessmentTools.name: Transparency Reporting
assessmentTools.description: Generates transparency reports and notifications for AI system users and affected persons.

assessmentTools.name: Incident Response
assessmentTools.description: Framework for identifying, reporting, and responding to AI system incidents and serious incidents.

assessmentTools.name: Model Cards Generator
assessmentTools.description: Automated generation of comprehensive model cards documenting AI system capabilities, limitations, and performance.

documents.name: Risk_Management_Framework.pdf
documents.description: Risk assessment and management framework for AI systems

prohibitedPractices.label: Subliminal / manipulative / deceptive techniques that materially distort behaviour and are likely to cause significant harm
Exploitation of vulnerabilities (age, disability, or social/economic situation) to distort behaviour likely causing significant harm
Social scoring leading to detrimental/unfavourable treatment (esp. unjustified/disproportionate)
Criminal offence risk assessment / prediction based solely on profiling or personality traits (individual predictive policing)
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is used to support a human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to criminal activity (not solely profiling). (Art.5(1)(d))

prohibitedPractices.label: Untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV to build / expand facial recognition databases
Emotion recognition in the workplace or in education settings
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is for medical or safety reasons. (Art.5(1)(f))

prohibitedPractices.label: Biometric categorisation that infers or predicts sensitive traits (e.g., race, political opinions, religion, trade union membership, sexual orientation)
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is for labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets, such as images, based on biometric data or categorizing of biometric data in the area of law enforcement. (Art.5(1)(g))

prohibitedPractices.label: Real-time remote biometric identification (RBI) in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: Only if strictly necessary for one of the listed objectives (victims / imminent serious threat / serious crime suspect) and with safeguards + authorisation requirements (Art. 5(2)–(7)).

highRiskCategories.category: Biometric identification and categorisation of natural persons
highRiskCategories.description: Remote biometric identification systems and biometric categorisation systems

highRiskCategories.category: Management and operation of critical infrastructure
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of road traffic and supply of water, gas, heating and electricity
AI systems intended to be used for determining access to educational institutions, evaluating learning outcomes, or assessing appropriate level of education

highRiskCategories.category: Employment, workers management and access to self-employment
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for recruitment, making decisions affecting employment, task allocation, and monitoring/evaluation of persons in work-related contractual relationships

highRiskCategories.category: Access to and enjoyment of essential private services and public services and benefits
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to evaluate creditworthiness, assess eligibility for public assistance, or dispatch emergency services
AI systems intended for individual risk assessment, lie detection, emotion recognition, deep fake detection, or evaluating evidence reliability

highRiskCategories.category: Migration, asylum and border control management
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended for assessing risk posed by persons, examining applications, or detecting irregular migrants

highRiskCategories.category: Administration of justice and democratic processes
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to assist judicial authorities in researching and interpreting facts and law

regulatoryFrameworks.name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
regulatoryFrameworks.description: Regulation on data protection and privacy

regulatoryFrameworks.name: Data Act
regulatoryFrameworks.description: Regulation on harmonised rules on fair access to and use of data

regulatoryFrameworks.name: Digital Services Act (DSA)
